subject: Public Health
title: Alcohol and Nutrition
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Alcohol and Nutrition.

hook.text: Alcohol provides calories but zero nutrition—and it actively blocks the absorption of essential vitamins.

buildup.text: Heavy drinking depletes B vitamins, folate, and zinc while damaging the liver's ability to process nutrients.

discovery.text: Wernicke-Korsakoff syndrome—severe brain damage from thiamine deficiency—is a direct result of alcoholism.

twist.text: Alcohol is classified as a Group 1 carcinogen—no safe level of consumption has been established.

climax.text: Alcohol contributes to malnutrition in wealthy and poor countries alike—cutting across income levels.

punchline.text: Alcohol feeds calories but starves the body of nutrients.

quiz.question: What brain condition results from alcohol-induced thiamine deficiency?
